---
title: "Small's Yellow-eyed Grass (Xyris smalliana)"
slug: "xyris-smalliana"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180846210/medium.jpeg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Small's Yellow-eyed Grass"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Xyridaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Xyridaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180846210/medium.jpeg" alt="Small's Yellow-eyed Grass" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/almeat557">
almeat557</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/107497508">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Xyridaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=170364)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Uncommon in Florida found in moist soil, ditches, and pond margins.

## Natural history notes

A slender wetland plant characterized by its narrow, grass-like leaves and small yellow flower heads borne on upright stalks. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and savannas, often in sandy soils. It typically blooms during the warmer months and is most noticeable when in flower, attracting small pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/170375-Xyris-smalliana)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/2488)
